narrative.headline: Elbit Systems wins $712M in contracts amid Israeli lobbying surge
narrative.body: Elbit Systems announced $435M and $277M contracts from undisclosed international customers on consecutive days in February 2026. The awards follow a cluster of Israeli-linked FARA/LDA filings spanning defense, cybersecurity, and media advocacy.
connections: NSO Group lobbying via Chartwell Strategy Group
Kela Defense US advocacy registration, February 2026
A. Halper Ltd. public advocacy through Washington Global Advisors
Elbit Systems dual NASDAQ/TASE listing, international customer anonymity

narrative.headline: UK lobbying activity spikes 3σ above baseline in 30 days
narrative.body: Five discrete lobbying and defense signals tied to the United Kingdom emerged in 30 days against a 1.2-signal average, spanning AUKUS submarine systems, AI risk, defense appropriations, and Formula One event security. The cluster combines a $1B DSCA arms sale with four LDA registrations across unrelated sectors.
connections: AUKUS vertical deployment tube procurement
FY27 defense appropriations lobbying, UK-linked firm
Artificial superintelligence risk advocacy, US Congress
Formula One federal event security and visa issues

narrative.headline: Managed money wheat shorts hit 2.8σ anomaly with AU, CA, RU active
narrative.body: CFTC data shows managed money net short 994 wheat contracts, 2.8 standard deviations above baseline as of March 24, 2026. Three of six exposed countries — Australia, Canada, Russia — show concurrent lobbying signals across defense, agriculture, and trade.
connections: Manildra Milling lobbying trade and agriculture, January 2026
Iluka Resources defense lobbying, March 2026
Wheat export exposure: RU, AU, CA overlap
Managed money extreme short positioning in soft commodities

narrative.headline: 31 lobbying firms active on Canada issues within 371 days
narrative.body: Algorithm flags unusual concentration of 31 distinct firms filing on Canadian-linked issues, spanning energy, defense, and foreign affairs. Clients include pipeline, quantum computing, graphite, and hydrogen energy interests seeking U.S. congressional and regulatory access.
connections: Enbridge pipeline permitting reform lobbying
D-Wave quantum computing defense appropriations
Alabama Graphite critical minerals defense supply chain
Snow Lake Energy congressional outreach, Canada hydrogen

narrative.headline: 27 lobbying firms active on Japan-linked issues in under a year
narrative.body: Twenty-seven distinct firms filed on Japan-related matters across 369 days, spanning pharma pricing, trade tariffs, critical minerals, and steel investment. The breadth signals coordinated Japanese corporate exposure to U.S. policy shifts.
connections: Japanese pharma firms under U.S. drug pricing pressure
Toyota Tsusho critical minerals and green energy policy
U.S. Steel capital investment regulatory scrutiny
Daiichi Sankyo trade and tariff lobbying activity

narrative.headline: Canadian lobbying cluster precedes U.S. defense sale by seven days
narrative.body: A FARA filing on 2025-09-24 preceded a DSCA notification on 2025-10-01 by seven days, fitting a documented influence-to-military sequence pattern. Contributing filings span Canadian trade, critical minerals, and defense manufacturing lobbying.
connections: Bombardier tariff exposure and U.S. investment lobbying
Tenstorrent defense manufacturing advocacy
Rare earth and critical minerals supply chain filings
Canadian auto and axle trade tariff pressure

narrative.headline: 17 lobbying firms registered on Australia issues in 378 days
narrative.body: Seventeen distinct firms filed U.S. lobbying disclosures touching Australian interests within a 378-day window, spanning defense tech, rare earths, wheat tariffs, and energy. The concentration signals sustained, multi-sector Australian commercial and government engagement with U.S. federal agencies.
connections: Fivecast Pty Ltd — defense and intelligence appropriations
Iluka Resources — rare earth minerals funding advocacy
DroneShield — counter-UAS systems lobbying
Manildra Milling — Australia-specific wheat gluten tariffs

narrative.headline: 26 lobbying firms active on UK issues in under a year
narrative.body: LDA filings show 26 distinct firms registered on UK-related issues within 363 days, spanning defense, AI risk, pharma, patents, and event security. Breadth of sectors signals elevated post-Brexit and AUKUS-adjacent U.S.-UK policy friction.
connections: AUKUS submarine systems DSCA approval, March 2026
GSK pharmaceutical trade and tariff lobbying
Formula One Group federal visa and security issues
Control AI US artificial superintelligence risk advocacy

narrative.headline: UK-linked lobbying preceded $200M military terminal sale by one day
narrative.body: An LDA filing on AI risk issues was logged March 19, 2026, one day before a DSCA approval for a $200M Navy Multiband Terminal sale to the UK. The temporal sequence across influence and military procurement layers flags a recurring pattern in UK-related filings.
connections: $200M Navy Multiband Terminal sale to UK
Control AI US lobbying on superintelligence risks
Guardian Metal Resources UK defense supply chain advocacy
EZ Lynk UK patent issues via Checkmate Government Relations

narrative.headline: 16 lobbying firms registered on China-linked issues in 322 days
narrative.body: Sixteen distinct firms filed China-related lobbying disclosures spanning tariffs, CFIUS reviews, technology regulation, and supply chains. The concentration signals broad corporate hedging across trade, finance, and manufacturing policy fronts.
connections: CFIUS review activity, Grand Pharmaceutical Group
GE Appliances supply chain lobbying via Mehlman Consulting
Arashi Vision technology regulation exposure
Primemax Medical tariff mitigation efforts
